findings.title: PROP-061 Step 4c has two distinct bugs producing silent failure mode; root cause is LOCAL_SHA != REMOTE_SHA being the wrong trigger for git→FUSE sync
findings.summary: PROP-061's Step 4c git→FUSE auto-sync has never executed successfully in the 40 workspace-sync cycles since deployment 2026-05-27T19:56Z. Zero sync-workspace-runs-*.json success sentinels exist. One non-FF abort sentinel (2026-05-28T21:07Z) was a false-positive caused by Step 4b silent push failure. One divergence sentinel (2026-05-29T11:07Z) was overridden by LLM with custom note that correctly diagnosed but didn't fix the false-positive class. All other cycles silently exited via the LOCAL == REMOTE early-return branch because Step 1's `git pull --rebase` silently fast-forwards LOCAL to match REMOTE before Step 2 runs, making the SHA comparison meaningless for detecting FUSE-vs-upstream divergence.
evidence_files: monitor/integrity/workspace-sync-runs/run-2026-05-28T17-07-15Z.json through monitor/integrity/workspace-sync-runs/run-2026-05-29T10-07-20Z.json (18 cycles inspected; all `agent_notes` contained '45-49 mtime-guard skips (git newer than FUSE — normal)' — the agent was acknowledging the bug as 'normal' on every cycle)
findings.operator_observed_symptom: FUSE monitor/prompts/curmudgeon.md = 518 lines, no override clause, vs git origin/main 521 lines with override clause present. Operator-direct push of override clause at 2026-05-28T17:19Z (commit e4f90e75e1) sat un-propagated to FUSE for ~17 cycles / ~17 hours.
findings.remediation_path: monitor/tinker/proposals/PROP-064-prop-061-step-4c-silent-skip-fix.json — recommends option B (SHA-comparison fix: capture PRE_PULL_SHA/POST_PULL_SHA at Step 1, replace binary LOCAL==REMOTE check with four-way merge-base classification, treat REMOTE-ancestor-of-LOCAL as benign no-op). Phase 1 ships A+B together (~30 lines). Phase 2 adds Step 4b push-visibility hardening + structure-integrity FUSE-staleness audit.

directive_work_steps_covered.step_1_root_cause_diagnosis: Complete. See PROP-064 problem.root_cause_diagnosis (Bug A + Bug B both characterized).
directive_work_steps_covered.step_2_reconstruct_2107z_abort: Complete. See PROP-064 problem.the_2026_05_28T21_07Z_abort_reconstruction. Sequence inferred from skip log evidence + run report consistency + missing/present-in-FUSE asymmetry of sentinel files.
directive_work_steps_covered.step_3_surface_silent_skip_path: Complete. The silent skip is not from `set -e` missing or trap eating errors — it's the binary `if LOCAL==REMOTE` returning true after every successful push. Bug A is a logic-correctness defect, not an execution-failure defect. Identified in PROP-064 problem.root_cause_diagnosis.bug_A_silent_skip_when_pull_moved_head.
directive_work_steps_covered.step_4_recommend_a_fix: Complete. PROP-064 authored at monitor/tinker/proposals/PROP-064-prop-061-step-4c-silent-skip-fix.json. Recommends option B (SHA-comparison fix); alternatives A/C/D analyzed.
directive_work_steps_covered.step_5_operator_visibility_audit: Authored as PROP-064 workstream C (structure-integrity.md md5-compare FUSE vs git for OWNERSHIP+prompts on daily cadence). Phase 2 follow-up, separable from Phase 1 main fix.

context_load.ad_hoc_observation: PROP-061 itself was stale in FUSE (FUSE 2026-05-27T14:43Z vs git 2026-05-28T17:07Z) — the proposal that was supposed to fix git→FUSE staleness was, ironically, itself a victim of the bug it was about. This is a useful concrete example for future PROP authoring about why writer/consumer asymmetry creates compounding debt.
